caption:
I had some of the best bites of pasta in my entire life! Eating at fileja was both a delicious and riveting experience. I enjoyed watching the chef meticulously craft each piece of pasta while also making sure that they were cooked perfectly al dente. It’s been a few months since my visit, but I still think about the pasta here.⁣
⁣
The courses in their pasta omakase do change based on the season. I hope to come back again in the future to try even more pasta. Thank you to @roaming.eater for the amazing recommendation!⁣
⁣
📍 fileja (@fileja2021)⁣
Japan, 〒101-0041 Tokyo, Chiyoda City, Kanda Sudacho, 2 Chome−13−1 ノルン秋葉原ビル 1階階⁣
⁣
⁣
⁣
#pasta #italianfood #tokyofood #tokyotrip #omakase
transcript: Come with me to a 15-course pasta omakase in Tokyo, Japan. This is Phileya, a small eight-seat pasta restaurant located on a quiet street in Akihabara. My husband and I had dinner reservations to try their 15-course tasting menu, with eight of them featuring a variety of pastas that are all freshly crafted right in front of you. It was so interesting seeing all the different tools around the kitchen and how they were used to create the many pasta shapes. Every single pasta was cooked perfectly al dente, and I love trying all the different flavor combinations. I enjoyed every single bite here, and my favorite pasta courses included the creamy truffle and crab shagliatelle, the rich lamb ragu gnocchi, and the savory long tomato pasta. Some of the non-pasta courses included sea bream, this beautifully braised beef cheek, and this squid and broccoli dish that kind of looked like pasta too. Additionally, I was extra impressed that our whole dinner was prepared and served by one person the entire time. Truly a master in multitasking. This was some of the best pasta I've ever had, and I'll definitely be back again in the future.

transcript_segments.text: Come with me to a 15-course pasta omakase in Tokyo, Japan.
This is Phileya, a small eight-seat pasta restaurant located on a quiet street in Akihabara.
My husband and I had dinner reservations to try their 15-course tasting menu,
with eight of them featuring a variety of pastas that are all freshly crafted right in front of you.
It was so interesting seeing all the different tools around the kitchen
and how they were used to create the many pasta shapes.
Every single pasta was cooked perfectly al dente, and I love trying all the different flavor combinations.
I enjoyed every single bite here, and my favorite pasta courses included the creamy truffle and crab shagliatelle,
the rich lamb ragu gnocchi, and the savory long tomato pasta.
Some of the non-pasta courses included sea bream, this beautifully braised beef cheek,
and this squid and broccoli dish that kind of looked like pasta too.
Additionally, I was extra impressed that our whole dinner was prepared and served by one person the entire time.
This was some of the best pasta I've ever had, and I'll definitely be back again in the future.
